<!--
Thanks for contributing to SVG Peek!

Please make the PR easy to review:
  - Keep the scope tight. One logical change per PR.
  - Use Conventional Commits in the title: feat:, fix:, refactor:, docs:, test:, chore:
  - Link the issue this PR addresses.
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do, in 1–3 sentences? -->

## Related issue

<!-- Closes #123 -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking, fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking, adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would change existing behaviour)
- [ ] Documentation / chore

## How was this tested?

<!--
Describe the test cases you ran. For UI changes, attach a screenshot or short GIF.
If you added a new command or setting, list the files/languages you verified it on.
-->

## Checklist

- [ ] My commits follow Conventional Commits
- [ ] I ran `npm run check-types` and `npm run lint` locally and both pass
- [ ] I ran `npm test` locally and it passes
- [ ] I updated `CHANGELOG.md` under `## [Unreleased]`
- [ ] I updated the README / docs if user-facing behaviour changed
